label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from the Toy Fair 2014 based on the article provided. First, I need to recall the key points from the text. The article mentions that the 2014 Toy Fair had a trend called "Larger Than Life," which focuses on big toys with big play value. The expert Adrienne Appell says that in 2014, everything is really big and out there. Also, there was a push towards education with STEAM (science, tech, engineering, art, math), including word games and high-tech games like 3D and robots. Now, looking at the options: A) Toys are becoming more educational than fun. The article does mention a push towards education with STEAM, but it doesn't say they're becoming more educational than fun. It's a trend, but not necessarily replacing fun. So maybe not the main point. B) Robots are great favorites of buyers. The article says they saw some super cool, high-tech games and robots, and that technology influences toys. But "great favorites" might be an overstatement. It's mentioned as a trend, not necessarily that buyers love them the most. C) Big toys will be popular this year. The "Larger Than Life" trend is highlighted as a favorite, with Appell stating that everything is big. This seems directly supported by the text. D) Simple card
